image_upload.hint: JPEG, PNG or WebP, up to 2 MB.
inbox.title: Messages
inbox.subtitle: Conversations about tickets and extra work.
notifications.sa_empty_hint: Empty is intentional here: super admins receive no automatic notifications by default (only messages directed at you). Subscribe per company via the company filter above to receive that company's stream.
notifications.company_view_hint: Read-only view of this company's notification stream (one row per event).
inbox.empty_description: There are no messages in your inbox yet.
billing.title: Invoices
billing.subtitle: Billable extra work per month, grouped by customer.
billing.multi_company_hint: Multiple provider companies in view — narrow the view to run the invoice action.
billing.confirm_mark_body: This marks every earned, not-yet-invoiced extra-work item billing in {{month}} for this provider company as invoiced ({{count}} item(s) currently in view). You can undo this afterwards.
billing.confirm_clear_body: This clears the invoiced mark for every extra-work item billing in {{month}} for this provider company ({{count}} item(s) currently in view).
billing.empty_desc: No billable extra work in the selected month with these filters.
customer_subpage_placeholder.title: Coming soon
customer_subpage_placeholder.description: This section will be implemented in a future sprint. The navigation foundation ships first.
route_badge.instant_helper: All requested services were already priced — tickets were spawned immediately.
route_badge.proposal_helper: At least one line had no agreed price — needs a provider proposal before any work begins.
brand.name: CleanOps
provider_only_zone.label: Provider-only
provider_only_zone.hint: This section is not visible to customer users.
subject: Subject
sla_tooltip_historical: This ticket predates the SLA engine.
sla_tooltip_paused: Waiting on customer; SLA clock paused.
invitations.form_description: The invitee receives an email with a one-time link. They set their own password when accepting and join with the role and scope you pick here.
invitations.expires_note: Invitation links expire after 7 days. Pending invitations can be revoked at any time.
invitations.empty_desc: When you invite teammates, they'll appear here grouped by status.
invitations.title: Invitations
invitations.buildings_hint: Pick one or more buildings the invitee will manage.
invitations.customers_hint: Pick one or more customers the invitee will manage.
invitations.dialog_revoke_body: The invitation link will stop working immediately. You can send a new one if you change your mind.
admin.empty_filtered_desc: Try clearing filters or switching the status tab.
users.empty_initial_desc: Invite the first user to get started.
companies.empty_initial_desc_admin: Create the first company to get started.
companies.empty_initial_desc_other: Ask a super admin to create one.
buildings.empty_initial_desc: Create the first building to get started.
customers.empty_initial_desc: Create the first customer to get started.
user_form.card_label_desc: Email, name, language and role for this user.
user_form.role_disabled_actor: (only super admins can manage this role)
user_form.role_staff_helper: Field staff users are managed from the staff profile and per-building staff-visibility flow under each staff user's profile, not from this role dropdown. Use Add user → Customer user / Building manager here; staff creation goes through invitations or seed scripts.
user_form.memberships_desc: Read-only summary. Memberships are managed from the entity detail pages.
user_form.dialog_deactivate_body: They will lose access immediately. Their data and history are kept; a super admin can reactivate them later.
user_form.dialog_reactivate_body: Reactivating restores their account. They will regain access using their existing password (or can request a reset).
company_form.forbidden_desc: Only super admins can create new companies. Ask one to create it for you, or edit an existing company below.
company_form.slug_placeholder: Leave blank to auto-generate from the name
company_policy.section_desc: What this provider company's admins are allowed to do. These grants are set by a super admin only.
company_policy.readonly_hint: Only a super admin can change these policies.
company_policy.manage_cca_helper: Lets provider admins grant or revoke the customer-company-admin access role.
company_policy.manage_catalog_helper: Lets provider admins edit the shared service catalog (categories, services).
company_policy.manage_prices_helper: Lets provider admins set customer-specific contract prices.
company_policy.quote_override_label: May start work bypassing customer quote approval
company_policy.quote_override_helper: DANGEROUS: lets a provider admin publish and start extra work without the customer approving the quote. Every use is recorded as a high-severity audit event.
company_form.section_admins_desc: Company admins linked to this company. Add an existing user below; new users come in via invitations.
company_form.dialog_deactivate_body: It will be hidden from non-super-admin users. Tickets attached to it remain visible to staff.
company_form.dialog_reactivate_body: Reactivating restores it for all roles. Existing memberships and tickets are unchanged.
company_form.dialog_remove_body: Their other memberships are unaffected. They can be re-added later.
building_form.card_label_desc: Company, name and address of this location.
building_form.section_managers_desc: Building managers assigned to this building. Add an existing user below; new users come in via invitations.
building_form.dialog_deactivate_body: It will be hidden from non-super-admin users. Tickets attached to it remain visible to staff.
building_form.dialog_reactivate_body: Reactivating restores it for all roles. Existing memberships and tickets are unchanged.
building_form.dialog_remove_body: Their other memberships are unaffected. They can be re-added later.
customer_form.section_users_desc: Customer users linked to this customer. Add an existing user below; new users come in via invitations.
customer_form.dialog_deactivate_body: It will be hidden from non-super-admin users. Tickets attached to it remain visible to staff.
customer_form.dialog_reactivate_body: Reactivating restores it for all roles. Existing memberships and tickets are unchanged.
customer_form.dialog_remove_body: Their other memberships are unaffected. They can be re-added later.
customer_form.section_buildings_desc: Buildings this customer operates at. A ticket can only be raised for a customer-and-building pair that is linked here.
customer_form.dialog_unlink_building_bod: Customer-user access for this building under this customer will also be revoked. Tickets already created for this pair stay in the audit log.
customer_form.access_no_buildings: No building access yet — this user cannot see any tickets under this customer.
customer_form.dialog_revoke_access_body: They will no longer see tickets at this building under this customer. Other building access is unaffected.
customer_form.access_active_hint: Switch off to suspend this building access without deleting it. The user keeps the row in their history; the resolver treats every permission as False while inactive.
customer_form.access_overrides_section_h: Pick a value per permission key. Inherit follows the role default and the customer-company policy; Grant or Revoke is an explicit per-user override. Inactive access denies every permission regardless of overrides.
customer_form.access_overrides_self_edit: You cannot edit your own access row.
customer_form.access_overrides_inactive_: This access is inactive — every permission resolves to False until you switch Active back on.
customer_form.policy_helper: Policy switches narrow what every customer-side user under this customer may do. Per-user overrides above can re-enable a permission for one user even when the policy disables it for the company.
customer_form.policy_field_create_ticket: Allow customer users to create tickets
customer_form.policy_field_approve_ticke: Allow customer users to approve ticket completion
customer_form.policy_field_create_extra_: Allow customer users to create extra work
customer_form.policy_field_approve_extra: Allow customer users to approve extra-work pricing
accept_invitation.missing_token_desc: The link in your email did not include a token. Open the email and click the link again, or ask the person who invited you for a fresh invitation.
accept_invitation.not_found_desc: The invitation token does not match anything on file. It may have already been used or revoked.
accept_invitation.gone_desc: This invitation is {{status}}. Ask the person who invited you to send a new one.
accept_invitation.welcome_lead: {{inviter}} invited you to join CleanOps as {{role}}.
accept_invitation.password_requirements_: Use at least 8 characters. Avoid very common or all-numeric passwords.
accept_invitation.error_passwords_dont_m: The two passwords do not match.
accept_invitation.error_user_exists_desc: An account with this email already exists. Sign in with your existing password.
accept_invitation.error_generic_desc: We couldn't accept the invitation. Please try again or contact support.
error_boundary.title: Something went wrong
error_boundary.description: An unexpected error stopped the page from loading. Reload to try again. If this keeps happening, please contact support.
audit_logs.title: Audit log
audit_logs.intro: Every create, update, and delete on tracked records lands here. Filter by record type, record id, or actor to investigate a specific change.
audit_logs.timeline_lookup_desc: Look up the complete audit trail for one ticket — status changes, audit events, Extra Work, and planned occurrences in one feed.
audit_logs.timeline_lookup_empty: No timeline events for this ticket yet.
audit_logs.timeline_lookup_not_found: No ticket #{{id}} found in your scope.
audit_logs.empty_initial_desc: Entries appear here automatically as soon as a tracked record is created, updated, or deleted.
audit_logs.empty_filtered_title: No audit entries match your filters
audit_logs.empty_filtered_desc: Try widening the date range or clearing the filters.
api_error.session_expired: Your session expired. Sign in again to continue.
api_error.not_allowed: You don't have permission to perform this action.
api_error.not_found: We couldn't find what you were looking for. It may have been deleted or moved.
api_error.invalid_input: Some of the values you entered are not valid. Please check the form and try again.
api_error.too_many_requests: Too many requests in a short time. Please wait a moment and try again.
api_error.server_unavailable: The server is having trouble right now. Please try again in a few seconds.
api_error.network: We can't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.
api_error.unexpected: Something unexpected happened. Please try again.
staff_requests.title: Staff assignment requests
staff_requests.intro: Staff request assignment to a ticket they are eligible to work on. Building managers and provider admins review the queue here.
staff_requests.empty_desc: Staff requests appear here when a field-staff member asks to be assigned to a ticket they have building visibility for.
staff_requests.empty_filtered_desc: Switch to 'All' to see requests that have already been approved, rejected, or cancelled.
staff_requests.reviewer_note_placeholder: e.g. assigned to the Tuesday crew
staff_requests.review_dialog_approve_int: Approving assigns {{staff}} to ticket {{ticket}}. They'll be able to work on it immediately.
staff_requests.review_dialog_reject_intr: Rejecting closes {{staff}}'s request for ticket {{ticket}}. They'll be free to request other tickets.
customer_form.contact_visibility_helper: Choose what the customer’s users see about the {{companyName}} field staff assigned to their tickets. Switching everything off shows a generic “Assigned to the {{companyName}} team” label instead.
customer_form.contact_visibility_helper_: Choose what the customer’s users see about the field staff assigned to their tickets. Switching everything off shows a generic “Assigned to the field-staff team” label instead.
staff_admin.profile_desc: Contact details and operational flags for this staff member. Customers see only the fields you enable on the customer record.
staff_admin.field_internal_note_placehol: Scheduling notes, never shown to customers.
staff_admin.field_can_request_assignment: Allow this staff to request assignment
When off, the staff member cannot send 'I want to do this work' requests for any building.
staff_admin.field_is_active_hint: Turning this off pauses the staff member operationally. Their account stays signed-in capable; use Deactivate above to fully disable login.
staff_admin.visibility_title: Buildings this staff can work in
staff_admin.visibility_desc: Each building below grants this staff member read access to its tickets and lets them request assignment on them. Add or remove buildings to change where they can operate.
staff_admin.visibility_no_rows: No buildings yet — this staff member only sees tickets they are directly assigned to.
staff_admin.visibility_can_request_hint: Per-building override. When the staff profile flag above is off, this stays off too.
staff_admin.dialog_remove_body: {{email}} will lose visibility on this building's tickets immediately. Already-assigned tickets stay assigned.
staff_admin.routes_to_customer_hint: When on, this staff member's IN_PROGRESS -> completed transitions on this building skip the manager-review gate and go straight to customer approval.
employees.explainer: Everyone on the provider side — admins, building managers, and field staff. Use the filters to narrow the list; open Manage account for full account settings.
employees.empty_initial_desc: Provider-side users appear here as soon as they are created.
customer_employees.edit_modal_subtitle: Set the access role for {{name}} per building. Changes apply immediately.
customer_employees.edit_no_access: This user has no per-building access rows yet.
customer_employees.empty_initial_desc: Users with login access at this customer appear here.
customer_people.manage_modal.title: Manage {{user}}
customer_people.manage_modal.not_found: This user is no longer a member of this customer.
customer_people.company_admin.all_buildi: Company admin — access to all buildings.
customer_people.company_admin.access_not: A company admin has access to all buildings. Per-building access is managed automatically — remove company admin to set building-level access.
customer_people.company_admin.self_warni: You cannot change your own company-admin status.
customer_people.company_admin.demote_con: They keep their user account but lose company-wide admin access. You can then grant per-building access if needed.
customer_people.company_admin.toast_revo: {{user}} is no longer a company admin.
my_employees.subtitle: Colleagues at your organisation with login access.
my_employees.empty_no_customer_desc: Your account is not linked to a customer organisation, so there is no employee directory to show.
customer_contacts.filter_search_placehol: Search by name, email, phone or role…
customer_contacts.empty_description: Add the first contact for this customer. Contacts are phone-book entries — they do not have login access.
customer_contacts.field_building_optiona: Optional — leave empty for company-wide contact
customer_contacts.promote_button_hint: Give this contact login access. An existing user with this email is linked, otherwise an invitation is sent to set a password.
customer_contacts.manage_permissions_hin: Edit this user's per-building access and permissions, including whether they can invite others.
customer_contacts.permissions_panel_empt: This user has no building access yet.
Grant access via the full permission matrix.
customer_contacts.permissions_panel_matr: Add or remove buildings in the full matrix.
customer_contacts.promote_modal_subtitle: Promote this contact to a customer user. Their contact details are kept; only login access is added.
customer_contacts.promote_email_help: If a user with this email already exists they are linked; otherwise an invitation email is sent to this address.
customer_contacts.promote_field_building: Pre-filled from the contact's buildings. Leave all unchecked to use the default.
customer_contacts.promote_no_buildings: No buildings are linked to this customer.
customer_contacts.promote_field_phone_hi: A valid Dutch (NL) phone number is required to create a user.
customer_contacts.promote_error_phone_re: A phone number is required to create a user.
customer_contacts.promote_error_phone_in: Enter a valid Dutch (NL) phone number.
services.bulk_raise_intro: Select services to raise or lower their default catalog price. This updates the quoting baseline; customer-specific prices are not affected.
services.bulk_raise_empty: No active services to adjust yet.
services.bulk_raise_error_percent_lower: A percentage lower must be below 100.
services.bulk_raise_amount_fixed_hint: Amount added to or subtracted from each default price.
services.bulk_raise_error_amount: Enter an amount greater than zero.
services.empty_services_description: Create the first service to expose it for tickets and extra-work requests.
services.empty_categories_description: Create a category before adding services — every service belongs to one.
services.create_category_first: Create at least one category before adding a service.
services.field_reference_price_hint: Reference only — the instant-ticket gate uses per-customer pricing, not this default.
services.delete_service_confirm_body: The service will be removed from the catalog. Existing pricing rows that reference it will need to be cleaned up.
services.delete_category_confirm_body: If the category still contains services it cannot be removed. Move the services to another category first.
customer_pricing.delete_confirm_body: This contract price will be removed. Future extra-work requests will fall back to the proposal phase until a new price is created.
customer_pricing.empty_description: Add the first contract price to unlock the instant-ticket path for this customer.
customer_pricing.field_service_locked_hi: Service cannot change after creation — delete this row and add a new one to switch service.
customer_pricing.field_valid_to_hint: Leave empty for an open-ended price.
customer_pricing.error_valid_to_before_v: Valid-to must be on or after valid-from.
customer_pricing.bulk_raise_intro: Select the prices you want to raise or lower. New price rows are created from the effective date; existing prices are kept for history.
customer_pricing.bulk_raise_empty: No active prices to adjust yet.
customer_pricing.bulk_raise_error_percen: A percentage lower must be below 100.
customer_pricing.bulk_raise_amount_fixed: Amount added to or subtracted from each selected price.
customer_pricing.bulk_raise_error_amount: Enter an amount greater than zero.
customer_pricing.copy_from_default_intro: Seed this customer's contract prices from the catalog default prices. Services that already have an overlapping active price are skipped.
customer_pricing.copy_from_default_empty: No active services in the catalog yet.
customer_pricing.copy_from_default_resul: Created {{created}} price(s), skipped {{skipped}} that already had one.
customer_custom_pricing.error_unit_label: Enter a unit name for the Other unit type.
customer_custom_pricing.delete_confirm_b: This archives the custom price line so it can no longer be used.
ticket_staff_complete.note_label_or_phot: Add a completion note or attach a photo of the completed work (required)
ticket_staff_complete.note_required_hint: A completion note or photo attachment is required.
ticket_staff_complete.note_or_photo_hint: Either a typed note OR an attached photo of the completed work satisfies this.
ticket_staff_complete.route_manager_revi: This will go to your manager for review.
ticket_staff_complete.route_customer_app: This will be sent to the customer for approval.
ticket_staff_complete.error_evidence_req: Please add a note or attach a photo before sending.
ticket_staff_complete.error_route_mismat: The routing changed. Please refresh and try again.
ticket_staff_complete.attachment_hint: If you have a photo, upload it from the Attachments card on this page before sending.
ticket_staff_complete.attachment_hint_sa: A photo is already attached — you can submit without typing a note.
bm_customers.title: Customers in your assigned buildings
bm_customers.readonly_hint: Read-only — you can view customers and their contacts in the buildings you manage.
bm_customers.empty: No customers are linked to your assigned buildings yet.
bm_customer_detail.buildings_empty: This customer is not linked to any buildings you can see.
bm_customer_detail.contacts_hint: View the contact persons for this customer in your assigned buildings.
bm_customer_contacts.title: Contacts
bm_customer_contacts.readonly_hint: Read-only — click a row to see the contact's details.
bm_customer_contacts.empty: No contacts have been registered for this customer.
customer_view.overview.explainer: This customer is served by your provider company at the linked buildings.
customer_view.permissions.title: Access and permissions
customer_view.permissions.subtitle: Manage which users can act on this customer and which permissions they have at each building.
customer_view.users.title: Users
customer_view.users.permission_hint: Edit per-building permissions in the Permissions tab.
customer_view.buildings.title: Linked buildings
customer_view.settings.title: Customer settings
customer_view.overview.explainer_with_pr: {{customer}} is served by {{provider}} at {{count}} linked building(s). This is the read-only home — pick a management area below.
customer_view.overview.explainer_generic: {{customer}} is served by your provider company at {{count}} linked building(s). This is the read-only home — pick a management area below.
customer_view.overview.buildings_preview: No buildings linked yet. Add the first one from the Buildings tab.
customer_view.overview.quicklink_contact: People at this customer you communicate with.
customer_view.overview.quicklink_buildin: Locations where this customer receives service.
customer_view.overview.quicklink_users_d: Login accounts for this customer's people.
customer_view.buildings.explainer: Linked buildings are the locations where {{customer}} receives service. Tickets and extra work can only be raised for the customer + building combinations listed here.
customer_view.users.explainer: Login accounts that can act on behalf of {{customer}}. Permissions and per-building access are managed on the Permissions tab.
customer_view.users.filter_no_matches: No users match the current filters.
customer_view.permissions.explainer: Access controls for {{customer}}. Use the Users tab to add or remove customer accounts; here you fine-tune per-building roles, per-permission overrides, and the customer-company policy that gates create / approve actions.
customer_view.extra_work.explainer: Extra-work requests raised for {{customer}}. Each row links to the full request detail where pricing, history, and decisions live.
customer_view.extra_work.empty_desc: Requests for additional services outside the standard contract appear here once raised.
customer_view.quote_requests.explainer: Quote requests raised for {{customer}}. Each row links to the full request where pricing and the quote decision live.
customer_view.quote_requests.empty_desc: Price-quote requests from this customer appear here.
my_meldingen.title: My reports
my_meldingen.subtitle: Reported issues and requests for your location.
my_meldingen.empty_no_customer_desc: Reports are available for customer accounts.
my_meldingen.empty_desc: Reports for your location will appear here.
customer_view.tickets.explainer: Tickets raised for {{customer}}. Each row links to the full ticket.
customer_view.tickets.empty_desc: Tickets for this customer appear here.
customer_view.meldingen.explainer: Customer reports for {{customer}}. Each row links to the full report.
customer_view.meldingen.empty_desc: Reports submitted by this customer appear here.
customer_view.settings.explainer: Customer-wide preferences. Changes apply to all users and staff acting for {{customer}}.
customer_view.settings.visibility_helper: Decides whether customer users see staff name / email / phone on ticket details. Other roles always see full staff contact info.
customer_view.settings.deactivate_conseq: Deactivating disables logins for this customer's users and hides them from default lists. Active tickets are NOT auto-closed.
customer_view.settings.reactivate_conseq: Reactivating restores logins and unhides this customer from default lists.
customer_permissions.zone_policy_helper: Policy switches narrow what every customer user at this customer can do. Per-user overrides below can re-enable a key for one user, even when the policy disables it for the company.
customer_permissions.zone_users_helper: Each user has a sub-role per building. Custom permissions let you tweak individual keys without changing the whole sub-role.
customer_permissions.modules_zone_helper: Enable or disable modules and their permissions for everyone at this customer. Fine-grained control lives under Advanced.
customer_permissions.modules.off_note: This module is off: its permissions are disabled for every user of this customer.
customer_permissions.modules.master_off_: This turns off all of this module's permissions for every user of this customer. You can still review the change before saving.
customer_permissions.advanced_helper: Per-permission policy, technical keys and per-user exceptions
customer_permissions.access_no_buildings: This user is a member of the customer but has no building access yet. Add one below.
customer_permissions.you_chip_hint: You cannot edit your own access. Ask another administrator if you need to be removed.
customer_permissions.overrides_drawer.ef: Effective: Deny (role default does not grant this)
customer_permissions.matrix.empty: No customer users have building access yet.
customer_permissions.overrides_drawer.se: You cannot edit your own access. Switch users to make changes here.
customer_permissions.overrides_drawer.in: This access is currently inactive — every key resolves to Deny until you re-activate it.
customer_permissions.access_panel.title: Effective permissions at {{building}}
customer_permissions.toast_save_failed: Could not save changes — please try again
customer_permissions.policy_card.create_: Customer users at this customer can open new tickets.
customer_permissions.policy_card.approve: Customer users can approve or reject ticket completion.
customer_permissions.policy_card.create_: Customer users can request extra work outside the standard contract.
customer_permissions.policy_card.approve: Customer users can approve or reject extra-work pricing proposals.
customer_permissions.permission_keys.cus: Open new tickets at this building.
See every ticket at this building, including ones other customer users created.
See every ticket across every building linked to this customer organisation.
Approve or reject completion on tickets this user created.
Approve or reject completion on any ticket at this building.
Request extra work outside the standard contract.
See extra work this user requested.
See every extra-work request at this building.
See every extra-work request across this customer organisation.
Approve or reject pricing proposals on extra work this user requested.
Approve or reject pricing proposals on any extra work at this building.
Send an invitation to a new customer user.
Edit profile and active state of other customer users at this customer.
Promote another customer user to location manager at a specific building.
Edit custom permissions of other customer users.
company_detail.admins_desc: People who can manage this company.
user_detail.memberships.title: Memberships
user_detail.memberships.desc: Companies, buildings, and customers this user belongs to.
user_detail.memberships.empty: This user has no scope memberships.
user_detail.customer_access.title: Customer access
user_detail.customer_access.desc: Customers this user can act on. Click View permissions to manage what they can do.
user_detail.bm_permissions.title: Building manager permissions
user_detail.bm_permissions.desc: Per-building toggles for the two revocable building-manager defaults. Super admin and the building's company admin can change them; everyone else sees this read-only.
user_detail.bm_permissions.read_only_not: Only super admin and the building's company admin can change these.
permissions_rollup.aria_default: Permissions follow the customer default. Click to view.
permissions_rollup.aria_custom_one: 1 custom permission override. Click to view.
permissions_rollup.aria_custom_other: {{count}} custom permission overrides. Click to view.
notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.subtitle: Status changes and events. Conversations live in Messages.
customer_reports.helper: Extra-work revenue for this customer, anchored on the billing month (earned work). Amounts follow the same rules as the Invoices page.
facturen.title: Invoices
facturen.subtitle: Generate and manage extra-work invoices per customer.
facturen.due_sub: Customers with a billing schedule and their unbilled extra work this period.
facturen.due_empty: No customers with a billing schedule.
facturen.list_empty_desc: No invoices match these filters yet.
facturatie.section_sub: Billing schedule and contract PDF for this customer.
facturatie.contract_hint: Informational only; no effect on billing. One active PDF.
invoice_detail.confirm_issue_body: A final invoice number is assigned. After issuing, the invoice can no longer be edited.
invoice_detail.confirm_send_body: The invoice will be marked as sent.
invoice_detail.confirm_reverse_body: A negative credit note is created and the linked extra work returns to 'unbilled'.
invoice_detail.confirm_delete_body: The draft is deleted and the linked extra work returns to 'unbilled'.
invoice_detail.confirm_remove_line_body_: The linked extra work returns to 'unbilled'.
This manual line will be removed.
invoice_detail.summary_hint: Hand-written summary at the top of the invoice. Leave empty for the automatic text.
invoice_detail.fee_hint: Free text + amount (0% VAT). Editable only while draft.
invoice_detail.readonly_note: This invoice has been issued and can no longer be edited.
customer_facturen.title: Invoices
customer_facturen.subtitle: Your sent invoices.
customer_facturen.empty_desc: You have no sent invoices yet.
